releases.name: v2.0.8

releases.author: anshuman-ecc-eth
releases.description:
Remove dead UI code and unused styles

- Removed orphaned `TokenInstructionsModal` (token-modal.ts) — unused delegation token flow
- Removed `userName` / display name setting — not used by any feature
- Removed "Open hyvmind settings" and "Refresh status" buttons — simplified to Request / Clear / Check / Confirm
- Removed `handleRefreshBinding()` dead method
- Pruned 12 unused CSS classes from styles.css
releases.name: v2.0.7

releases.author: anshuman-ecc-eth
releases.description:
## Fixed

- **Unbind no longer destroys the plugin identity**: The Ed25519 key is now preserved across unbind/reinstall. `bindingData` is no longer cleared. Only the local `boundUser` reference is removed.

## Added

- **Stale binding recovery**: "Check stale bindings" button calls the backend's `getBoundPluginKeys()` to list all plugin keys bound to your account. Old/orphaned keys can be individually revoked via "Revoke" buttons.
- **Backend methods**: `getBoundPluginKeys` (query) and `revokePluginBinding` (update) added to the canister interface.
releases.name: v2.0.6

releases.author: anshuman-ecc-eth
releases.description:
### Fix
After a successful upload, the plugin now opens hyvmind.app in your browser. This triggers a fresh page load, which runs the auto-import from `getNotesData()` into the Notes editor. After the page loads, navigate to the Notes tab and look in the file tree (right sidebar) — the imported folder and files should appear there.
releases.name: v2.0.5 - Open hyvmind.app after upload

releases.author: anshuman-ecc-eth
releases.description:
### Fix
The plugin was sending folder data with a separate `files` array, but the web app's `convertObsidianData` function expects all items (files and subfolders) in a single `folders` array. Files are distinguished from folders by having `content` set, while folders have `folders` set instead.

### How to update
1. Upload again — the data format is now correct
2. Refresh the hyvmind.app Notes page after uploading
3. The imported files should appear in the Notes editor

If the binding status still shows 'Not bound', use the 'Confirm binding manually' button with your principal.
releases.name: v2.0.4 - Fix upload data format for Notes import

releases.author: anshuman-ecc-eth
releases.description:
### Fix

Added a Manual Bind fallback in settings. If the automatic binding status check fails (backend returns false despite approval being confirmed on hyvmind.app), the user can paste their principal and click 'Confirm binding manually' to set the local binding state without a backend query.

### Changes
- Added 'Confirm binding manually' button and text field in Plugin Binding section
- Allows bypassing getPluginBindingStatus() backend check entirely

### How to use
1. Send binding request from plugin
2. Approve on hyvmind.app Settings
3. Click 'Refresh status' — if it still says 'No binding found', use the Manual Bind section instead
4. Your principal is already saved in the 'Your hyvmind principal' field above — just click 'Confirm binding manually'
releases.name: v2.0.3 - Manual bind fallback

releases.author: anshuman-ecc-eth
releases.description:
### Fix
The plugin had no way to learn that the binding was approved on the web app. The local state stayed "Not bound" even after the user approved on hyvmind.app.

### Changes
- Added "Refresh status" button in settings that calls getPluginBindingStatus() and syncs the local state
- Added auto-check on plugin load — if no local binding exists but the backend confirms binding is active, the plugin automatically updates to "Bound"
- Renamed setBoundUser to persistBoundUser for clarity

### How to use
1. Send binding request from plugin
2. Approve on hyvmind.app Settings
3. Click "Refresh status" in plugin settings — status updates to "Bound"
4. Or just restart Obsidian — auto-check picks it up
releases.name: v2.0.2 - Binding status sync (Refresh button + auto-check)

releases.author: anshuman-ecc-eth
releases.description:
### Fix
`saveSettings()` was calling `this.agent.updateConfig()` on every keystroke in any settings field, which destroyed the actor and set it to null. The binding request button then failed with 'actor not initialized'.

Removed the premature updateConfig call — the agent config only needs to be set once during `onload()`, and canisterId/host rarely change during normal use.
releases.name: v2.0.1 - Fix 'actor not initialized' error during binding

releases.author: anshuman-ecc-eth
releases.description:
### Breaking changes
Complete rewrite of the authentication model. No more Internet Identity delegation tokens. No AuthClient. No 30-day expiry.

### New auth flow
- Plugin generates an Ed25519 key pair on first launch
- User copies their hyvmind principal (from Settings) into the plugin
- Plugin calls `requestPluginBinding()` — anonymous, no auth needed
- User approves the binding in hyvmind.app Settings
- Plugin calls `storeNotesData()` directly with the Ed25519 identity

### Key changes
- `src/icp/auth.ts` — replaced ICPAuth with PluginBinding (Ed25519KeyIdentity management)
- `src/icp/agent.ts` — new IDL factory with binding + store methods
- `src/icp/uploader.ts` — simplified to produce folder JSON for Notes editor
- `src/settings.ts` — replaced delegation token UI with binding UI
- `src/main.ts` — simplified lifecycle, no disconnect flow
- `src/types/canister.ts` — cleaned up, only current interface methods
- `src/types/icp-sdk.d.ts` — added IDL.Principal to type declarations

### Required backend deployment
The backend canister must be updated with new methods before this release works.
releases.name: v2.0.0 - Simplified binding auth for direct Notes import

releases.author: anshuman-ecc-eth
releases.description:
### Breaking auth format change
The authentication token format has changed. Users must get a new token from hyvmind.app/obsidian-token.

### What changed
- **Token format**: The old format (only delegation chain) was insufficient — the delegation targeted the web app's session key, which the plugin couldn't use.
- **New format**: Exports both an Ed25519 key identity and its delegation chain as a combined `{ identity, delegation }` JSON blob.
- **Plugin reconstruction**: `importDelegationToken()` now manually constructs `Ed25519KeyIdentity.fromJSON()` + `DelegationChain.fromJSON()` + `DelegationIdentity.fromDelegation()` instead of relying on `AuthClient.create({ storage })`.
- **Type declarations**: Added `Ed25519KeyIdentity`, `DelegationChain`, and `DelegationIdentity` to `src/types/icp-sdk.d.ts` for proper TypeScript support.

### Files changed
- `src/icp/auth.ts` — Complete rewrite of `init()` and `importDelegationToken()`
- `src/types/icp-sdk.d.ts` — Added missing type declarations
- `src/settings.ts` — Updated placeholder text for new format
releases.name: v1.3.0 - Rewrite authentication for combined {identity, delegation} token format

releases.author: anshuman-ecc-eth
releases.description:
### Fixes
- **Token import** — fixed AuthClient storage format mismatch. The raw JSON string is now passed to storage instead of the parsed object, which the SDK's type guard requires.
- **Default canister ID** — updated from old project ID to the live backend canister `4p5ty-yyaaa-aaaam-qfana-cai`.

### Changes
- `src/icp/auth.ts`: Return raw delegation string (not parsed object) to custom storage in both `init()` and `importDelegationToken()`
- `src/settings.ts`: Default canister ID updated
releases.name: v1.2.1 - Fix token import and update default canister ID

releases.author: anshuman-ecc-eth
releases.description:
### Simplified authentication
- Added new token export page at hyvmind.app/obsidian-token
- Plugin now links directly to the web app for one-click token export
- No more IndexedDB/developer tools instructions
- Tokens expire after 30 days — revisit the page to renew
releases.name: v1.2.0 - Simplified authentication flow

releases.author: anshuman-ecc-eth
releases.description:
## What's New in v1.1.0

This release implements **manual token-based authentication** for Internet Identity, since Obsidian's embedded browser cannot open the system browser directly.

### Authentication Change

**Before:** The plugin attempted to open a popup for Internet Identity authentication.

**After:** Users now manually import a delegation token from their browser's id.ai session.

### How to Update

1. Download the new release files:
   - `main.js`
   - `manifest.json`
   - `styles.css`

2. Replace the files in your plugin folder

3. In Obsidian, go to **Settings → Hyvmind Uploader**

4. Follow the new authentication instructions to import your token

### New Features

- **Token expiry options** - Choose 1 day, 7 days, or 30 days validity
- **Token status display** - Shows when your token expires
- **Delete token** - Remove your token anytime from settings

### Bug Fixes

- Fixed authentication popup issue in Obsidian desktop
releases.name: v1.1.0 - Manual Token Authentication

releases.author: anshuman-ecc-eth
releases.description:
Initial release of Hyvmind Uploader plugin.

## Features
- Internet Identity authentication
- Upload folders to Hyvmind as source graphs
- Recursive folder scanning
- Batch upload for large payloads
- Progress tracking
- Status bar indicator

## Changelog
- Fixed LICENSE to proper MIT with Hyvmind copyright
- Fixed console.log in production code
- Fixed sentence case in settings headings
- Added comprehensive CSS with accessibility features
- Added ARIA attributes for screen reader support
- Added XSS sanitization for Notices

## Installation
Download main.js, manifest.json, and styles.css to your vault's plugins folder.
releases.name: Hyvmind Uploader v1.0.0
